diagnostics.headline: CS/min 8.7 vs your 2.3 norm — above your level

notes: Enemy had 1 healer; only 1 of you carried anti-heal — usually wants two vs a heal comp.
Their main damage was Renna (30k magical); 2/5 of you itemized magic resist.
Enemy ran 2/5 on or near their meta core.

closingNote: Closed slow — you led objectives but the game ran 35 minutes. Convert Fangtooth/tower leads into ending the game, don't farm the lead and risk a throw.
draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (support→offlane).

coaching.headline: Three favored lanes (carry, offlane, jungle) and a fed Sparrow carried it, but you let a 35-minute game stay close on bad trades.
coaching.team: The draft was strongly yours — carry, offlane and jungle all favored, and Sparrow turned it into 13 kills and 53k hero damage. You owned objectives (78k damage to 17k, 23 objective kills to 15). What kept it from being a clean win was a run of bad trades in the open: 0-2 at 8 min, 0-2 at 24.8, and the worst, a 4-man 0-4 at 27.8 with no objective up. You finally grouped properly — the 32.7 Orb Prime fight (2-0) and the 34.2 open-map ace (4-0) ended it. The closing note is right: convert Fangtooth and tower leads into ending the game instead of farming the lead and risking a throw. After 19 min you held every major; that should have closed sooner.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: A fed Sparrow (13 kills, 53k damage) plus winning the Orb Prime fight at 32.7 and the 34.2 ace — after bleeding bodies in open-map bad trades that dragged the game to 35 min.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Riktor was excellent — 17 assists, only 2 deaths, and big mitigation as the frontline. You set up the won fights at 12.2 and 34.2. The one fix is the open-map fight at 27.8 (0-4): a full 5v5 started with your mid pinned across the map and no major up. As the engage, that's the fight to wave off and ward instead.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Steel was favored into Sevarog and you locked the offlane (47k mitigated, 2 deaths). You showed up for the close — the 32.7 Orb Prime fight (2-0) was yours. You ran no armor into a 3-physical comp per the flag; with the game already won that didn't bite, but it's the habit to fix.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: This was the carry game — 13 kills, 2 deaths, 53k hero damage, and 32k into objectives. Your Sparrow was online from Vanquisher at 25 and you closed both ending fights. Nothing to fix in the play; the team just needed to group around you sooner to end before 35.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Feng Mao was the favored jungle and you delivered — 27k hero damage, 25k to objectives, and you anchored the early map. You got caught in the 24.8 and 27.8 bad trades though (0-2, 0-4). With the lead you had, those were the moments to take a tower or vision instead of flipping a coin-toss in the open.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Zinx was an unfavored lane (theirs all game vs Renna) and you still finished 6/3/9 with strong CS — good adaptation. You were in both close-out fights (32.7, 34.2). At 27.8 you were pinned and losing lane, so the team opened that 5v5 a body short across the map — that's the team's read to fix; keep poking from range.

notes: 🎮 private (carry) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 0-2 into a numbers advantage.
Xeebs (jungle) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 0-2 into a numbers advantage.
Mr_Meat31 (support) was alive but losing lane and pinned — with them stuck across the map, this was the wrong fight to start.
Goldilocks324 (offlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 0-2 into a numbers advantage.
Willy Guggenheim (midlane) was alive but losing lane and pinned — with them stuck across the map, this was the wrong fight to start.

threats: Chain-CC risk (1 hard-CC abilities, ~0.5s of lockdown): Grux Crush (knockup 0.5s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Greystone. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — you brought none.
synergy: Your engage/CC: Riktor Riplash (pull), Riktor Shock Therapy (silence 0.8s), Riktor Skewer (suppress 1.25s). Your kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
You have sustain (Riktor) — favor the longer fight where your heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Riplash pull → Shock Therapy silence → Skewer suppress ult
ourKits.winCondition: pull a target in and chain silence+suppress for a guaranteed kill
ourKits.playWith: burst that deletes his pulled/suppressed target
ourKits.playAgainst: respect Riplash range — stay spaced; cleanse/QSS the suppress

ourKits.combo: Bull Rush knockback → Force Shield wall → Shield Slam knockup+stun ult, Shield Bash %hp
ourKits.winCondition: peel the carry with walls/knockups or engage with Shield Slam
ourKits.playWith: a carry he can wall for and peel
ourKits.playAgainst: his Force Shield blocks projectiles — reposition around it; bait Shield Slam

ourKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
ourKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
ourKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
ourKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

ourKits.combo: Reaping Dash → Hamstring slows → Safeguard spellshield → Earth Shatter execute
ourKits.winCondition: trade with the spellshield up and execute low targets with Earth Shatter
ourKits.passive: permanent power on unique hero takedowns
ourKits.playWith: setup that holds a target for the execute
ourKits.playAgainst: bait Safeguard's spellshield before committing your key CC

ourKits.combo: Ricochet stun → Bad Medicine zone → Infuse heal an ally → Neural Regenesis revive
ourKits.winCondition: keep an ally alive with Infuse + the revive ult and poke with Ricochet
ourKits.playWith: a carry she can pocket-heal and revive
ourKits.playAgainst: she can't heal herself — dive HER; bait the revive before committing

enemyKits.combo: Smash 'n Grab pull → Crush knockup → Warlord's Challenge stun → bleed-stacked basics
enemyKits.winCondition: land the pull→knockup chain to lock a target while bleed and AS shred them
enemyKits.passive: basics stack a bleed for sustained damage
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his knockup/stun window
enemyKits.playAgainst: telegraphed engage — bait Crush/Pull, then punish; kite his slow start

enemyKits.combo: Assault The Gates leap → Make Way armor shred → Sacred Oath empowered basic → Stone Forged Soul stasis ult
enemyKits.winCondition: front-line aggressively and reset the fight with the stasis heal
enemyKits.playWith: a team that fights while he soaks and stasis-resets
enemyKits.playAgainst: don't waste burst into his stasis — wait it out; anti-heal cuts the ult heal

enemyKits.combo: Scar mark → Obliterate homing missiles → Reckoning 1v1 realm ult
enemyKits.winCondition: Reckoning a target into the Nether Realm and crit them down 1v1
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up if his 1v1 stalls
enemyKits.playAgainst: the ult is a 1v1 — peel the realmed ally can't help, so don't be caught alone; he's crit-reliant early-weak

enemyKits.combo: Phantom Rush → Subjugate root → Siphon → Colossal Blow
enemyKits.winCondition: stack Souls to snowball Siphon and lock down with the root
enemyKits.passive: ability kills grant permanent Soul stacks (HP + damage)
enemyKits.playWith: a team that converts his root into kills
enemyKits.playAgainst: deny his last-hits early to starve Souls; dodge the slow Subjugate

enemyKits.combo: Soulfire waves → Mortal Coil pull-zone → Stupor orb → Abyssal Rift zone
enemyKits.winCondition: zone a fight with Mortal Coil + ult and chunk from range
enemyKits.passive: consumes essences from kills for power
enemyKits.playWith: frontline to hold enemies in her zones
enemyKits.playAgainst: fragile and reliant on her zones — flank/dive around the telegraphs

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
